desc:
Jagged boulders and mounds of broken rock cover the floor of a cramped valley.
The piles of debris are precarious, prone to give way under the lightest
footfall. Scraggly firs prevail in scattered tracts of stable mountainside, and
the slopes are otherwise covered in twisted shrubs and brambles clinging to
patches of scree. A narrow cranny hides torn carcasses, ragged furs, and the
blackened ashes of a fire.
name: Rubble-Clogged Valley

desc:
Monstrous, broken slabs of rock crown a rugged peak. Their rough faces gaze
outward at the towering pine legions that coat the surrounding slopes. Lichen
and moss mask great swaths of the mountainsides, visible as vivid splotches of
green, orange, and red.
name: Jagged Mountaintop

desc:
A spiny ridge punctuated by massive protruding rock formations stretches between
two jagged peaks. While most of these protrusions are heavy slabs lodged deep in
the mountain, several boulders sit precariously atop narrow pillars carved by
erosion. Fir trees dominate the steep northern slope, which plunges into a murky
valley. The southern slope instead descends gently and is covered in scraggly
brambles which down to a tangled mass of gnarled oaks.
name: Forested Ridge

desc:
Jagged granite pillars crown a rugged summit that looms over its surroundings.
The pillars are enveloped by gnarled branches of black, vigorous trees that grow
on the peak. Sharp ridges covered in streaming strands of lichen extend to the
north and west. A sheer cliff face gazes south over the dim vastness of
Mirkwood.
name: Towering Peak

desc:
Sheer slopes fall away from a spiny ridge. Sharp boulders protrude at irregular
intervals, often walling off the entire crest of the ridge. The rock formations
are blanketed in slick grey lichen, adding further danger to the already
hazardous terrain.
name: Precarious Ridgeline

desc:
Hulking oaks with reaching branches gather in this hollow; mighty specimens at
the edges tangle with pines on the surrounding slopes. The crowns of the trees
join in an opaque cover of foliage. Down at the forest floor, undergrowth is
oddly absent, though low-hanging branches grasp at the open spaces between
trunks. Narrow, mossy runnels cut through the surrounding mountain-sides,
merging to form a stream that exits through a cleft to the west.
name: Twisted Hollow

desc:
Sheer mountainsides close in on this deep depression. Gnarled boughs of ancient
oaks intertwine thickly with each other. The oak roots are broad and mossy, and
a muffled droning issues from the dark voids beneath them. Rock pillars rising
from the forest floor pierce the canopy, allowing glimpses of the sky. A few
twisted hawthorns eke out an existence from the scraps of sunlight given them by
the pillars.
name: Twisted Hollow

desc:
A rocky streambed cuts a deep course through a sheer cleft in the mountainside.
Its smooth stones are covered in slick, jet black moss. Granite cliff walls
pocked with dark recesses rise to either side. Several massive boulders are
lodged far overhead, waiting for time and erosion to eventually cast them down.
name: Constricted Cleft

desc:
A sharp ridgeline leading from the south breaks up into a line of rounded
hillocks. Numerous caves pock the hillsides, dark recesses partly covered by
overhanging slabs. Young oaks grow on the crests of the mounds, but elderly
trees below them cast a permanent shadow over the slopes.
name: Forested Heights

desc:
A tall mound of rock rises in the centre of the valley. Broken branches bearing
withering leaves protrude from the pile. The western side is scorched and
blanketed in grey ash, and charred trees stretch down the valley in that
direction.
name: Burned Valley

desc:
Enormous dark oaks grow among the hills here with sprawling, exposed roots
creating impassable obstructions. Thorny brush grows on top of the treacherous
root mounds, scraping and scratching the hard gnarled roots as they plunge in
and out of the shrouded earth.
name: Forested Hill

desc:
Blackened trunks lie strewn across the valley. The underbrush is scarred as
well, charred remains peeking above the ashes. The devastation reaches halfway
up the slopes to either side, halted by rock faces barren of fuel. Tufts of
grass have begun to peek through the debris.
name: Burned Valley

desc:
Hulking trees stand tall upon a lonely hilltop, overseeing the rest of the
forest that rises away to the east. Long, snake-like green moulds spiral down
the base of the oaks and seem to become continuous with the giant roots that
creep from the base of the trees. To the north-west, a plateau looms far above
the forest.
name: Foreboding Wooded Hilltop

desc:
The mountainside is covered by blackened, ancient oaks. Many of the trees are
dried-out and leafless, while others are thick and lively though shadowy and
ominous still. Moss-covered boulders are scattered along the ground, some
obscured by small vegetation and all harboring some form of lichen.
name: Forested Mountainside

desc:
Stooped black oaks with overhanging ominous boughs grow in thick clumps
overhead. The moist-soil is peppered with pebbles and small rocks, and gaps
through the trees reveal that this part of the forest is on top of a low
mountain. To the north, the forest enters a few small rolling hills while to the
east and south, the treeline appears to slope down the mountain.
name: Forest on a Mountain

desc:
Towering black oaks and firs reach toward the sky as their mighty roots plunge
down into the dark earth along steep mountain slopes. Hooded mushrooms of red
and black colour grow along the roots, sapping nutrients from the very trees
that shelter them from the harshest sunlight of mid-day.
name: Steep Wooded Slope

desc:
Twisted trees grow in a disorderly fashion among steep hills, their warped forms
contorting around and along one another as they block out all light from above.
Beneath the battle of the trees, an ongoing struggle between different types of
small vegetation rages on. Vanquished heaps of dead plants lay rotting on the
forest floor, their decaying remains dotting the hillsides.
name: Forested Hill

desc:
Huge slabs of stone jut up from the earth like blunted knives, point toward the
sky in a strange and ominous fashion. Small ferns and moss grow on and among the
great stones which are otherwise overshadowed by overhanging pines and black
oaks. A small dark puddle has collected at the foot of one particularly large
rock slab. The rolling ground slowly descends to the west.
name: Dark, Mountainous Forest

desc:
Immense black oaks grow in a uniform pattern with an equidistant amount of space
between each tree across rugged mountain terrain. Great boughs spread out from
the trees above and interlock with one another, creating a ceiling of branches
that blots out the sky. The hard stone-filled soil is dry and packed with
pockets of gravel where the steep terrain permits small stones to gather.
name: Steep, Rugged Forest

desc:
Dark trees with hunched backs completely cover the rolling foothills of
Mirkwood, their branches crossing one another among tangled leaves and wiry
vines. Sharp spiny brush covers the forest floor to create a blanket of darkness
above a soft and springy moss-bed that makes up the hillsides themselves.
name: Hilly Forest

desc:
Great black oaks and scraggly vines dominate the darkened forest. The terrain is
uneven with large moss-covered boulders strewn about. To the south and east, the
ground climbs dramatically with many loose rocks and tangled roots making
passage impossible.
name: Forest among Mountainous Terrain

desc:
Thick, black trunks rise between moss-covered boulders amid terribly steep and
inhospitable mountainous terrain. An impenetrable canopy ensures that the forest
floor remains in perpetual gloom. The few small spaces between rough trunks and
jagged boulders are covered with overgrown vines and thorny creepers.
name: Dark Mountains

desc:
Malicious dark trees grow on top of a steep cliff that overlooks the forest to
the north and east. Overlapping slabs of grey and black stone cover the forest
floor, broken in various places by treacherous and unrelenting roots. Brush and
vegetation manage to grow in small cracks along the great stone slabs.
name: Wooded Mountainside

desc:
Great dark fir trees grow here with mighty roots spreading in every direction.
The sheer girth and height of the trunks and boughs serves to block out all
light from the sky above. Slimy, moss-covered boulders are interspersed along
the steeply-sloped forest floor, each entangled by long spindly vines that creep
from trunk to trunk.
name: Fir-infested Mountainside

desc:
The forest gradually slopes up to the north but is otherwise is encroached by
rounded mountains to the west and south. The only sign of life beyond the
towering trees comes in the form of black twisted fungi that snake their way up
the gnarled trunks. To the east, the forest becomes a dark narrow valley.
name: Mouldy Forest Valley

desc:
Tangled trees grow along the cliffside facing south, their branches interweaving
to block out all light from the sky above. Pools of black water gather at the
feet of the dark gnarled trunks, while thorny briars and pale, spotted mushrooms
lay strewn along the forest floor.
name: Tangled Cliffside Grove

desc:
Several narrow, steep-sided canyons cross the rugged Mountains of Mirkwood. One
such canyon becomes shallow enough to enter by stepping down several
moss-covered boulders suitably located for such a venture. Few trees grow here,
the rock being bare and largely free of soil. Tough shrubs and weeds still
manage to find plenty of cracks and fissures from which to sprout and flourish.
name: Canyon-riddled Mountains

desc:
Darkened oaks manage to grow on the rough ground dominated primarily by rock
slabs along the uneven terrain. Thick underbrush and tangly vines strangle all
other vegetation to the south and east, partially obscuring the sudden cliffs
that rise up in those directions.
name: Overgrown Mountainside

desc:
Blackened oaks and firs abruptly end before sheer cliffs that overlook the
forest valley to the north. The rare forest edge grants a view of the Mountains
of Mirkwood to the far east. To the south, the forest appears to continue into
the darkness while to the east, the ground descends steeply.
name: Cliff in the Forest

desc:
Charred stumps rise from a valley floor blanketed in powdery grey ash. Scorched
giant skulls are impaled on blackened poles lining a cleared path down the
centre of the valley. Steep rock walls close in to either side, making this a
natural bottleneck.
name: Blackened Outpost

desc:
A cascade of water falls from the southern slope of a charred valley. The
cataract flows into a stream clogged with ashes, blackened trunks, and large,
rotting furs. Scattered giant bones and massive crude clubs litter the valley,
evidence of a recent slaughter.
name: Blackened Outpost

desc:
The charred valley splits into two levels, the southern shelf rising several
feet above the northern. A stream of black, ash-laden water runs in from the
east and pools in the lower section, restrained by an obstruction further down
the valley. This stream has found an alternative outlet, trickling into a cave
in the northern mountainside. On the higher ledge, a ring of large rocks
surrounds a fire pit at the mouth of a gaping cavern.
name: Blackened Outpost

desc:
Massive boulders, blackened trunks, and other debris fill the centre of this
valley, damming off a stream flowing in from the east. The surrounding slopes
are covered in charred, disintegrating trees. Large swathes of missing
vegetation on the northern and western mountainsides reveal the origin of the
rockslide. Crude wheelbarrows rest next to piles of rubble gathered farther down
the valley, hinting at recent efforts to clear the obstruction.
name: Blackened Outpost

desc:
Wind and rain have cut into the mountainside, carving out a cleft that runs
north and east up the steep slope. Exposed rocks and boulders provide rough,
though relatively stable footing. Downslope of this natural trail, uprooted
trees and gaping scars in the mountain show evidence of a rockslide. Upslope,
scorched trees stand among the disintegrating ashes of burned vegetation.
name: Eroding Mountainside

desc:
An erosion-carved furrow divides contrasting terrain as it meanders up a steep
incline. Below, a vast part of the mountain slope has fallen away, leaving
exposed rocks and reddish dirt. Above, a great fire has left blackened shrubs
and shattered trees. Numerous rills trickle downward through the ashes, the
beginnings of a process that will send the mountainside cascading into the
valley below.
name: Eroding Mountainside

desc:
A narrow gap between two peaks shows signs of wear by the elements. Dry runnels
meander down the slopes, and there are broad swaths of missing vegetation now
occupied by mounds of unstable scree. The entire mountainside is scorched, and
there is no hint of green indicating regrowth of vegetation. Even the moss and
lichen have not recovered on these black and barren slopes.
name: Eroding Mountainside

desc:
A broad ledge curves around a mountain, leading to an overlook immediately to
the west. Large boulders have tumbled onto the ledge here and there, and gaps in
the ledge show where some have broken through. An unstable scree slope to the
east leads down to an isolated plateau covered in fir trees.
name: Ledge above a Plateau

desc:
This overlook affords an excellent view of a broad valley stretching from
northeast to southwest. Sharp, broken rock juts from either side of this crag,
and mounds of debris far below are evidence of its erosion. The surface is
somewhat unstable, and idle footsteps send small stones cascading down below.
Eagles' nests dot the slopes of a peak to the immediate south.
name: Rugged Overlook

desc:
Sheer, exposed rock lines the valley walls. A few charred trunks rest against
the cliffs, leaving scorch marks on the nearby rocks. A fire raged through here
in the past. Most of the soil is rocky and meagre and it does not look like
there was much plant life to begin with here that could burn.
name: Blackened Outpost

desc:
A natural arch spans a gap between sheer cliff walls. Skulls, giant skeletons,
and other trophies hang from crude nails embedded in the arch. A heavy gate
guards the valley to the east, and a recess in the northern cliff shelters a
stash of food and weapons. Grey ashes blanket the valley floor.
name: Blackened Outpost

desc:
Racks of weapons sit in the centre of a large chamber near the entrance to a
cave. Sets of leather and chain mail armour rest against the western wall, ready
to be donned at a moment's notice. The cavern branches as it cuts deeper into
the mountain; a passage to the east leads toward piles of crude bedding, while a
rotten stench betrays a slaughterhouse that lies around a bend in a tunnel to
the south.
name: Well-Stocked Armoury

desc:
Many burrows branch out from this cave, deeper into the mountain. Their darkest
recesses are well-shaded from daylight that might seep in from the entrance of
the cave. Furs from various animals are strewn across the floor: crude bedding
for the rank and file of the outpost. A burrow that is more spacious than the
others leads into the north wall of the chamber. A grate blocks off a niche in
the deepest part of the cave to the south; a wolf pen lies beyond it.
name: Sleeping Barrack

desc:
This burrow is spacious and relatively uncluttered, contrasting with a crowded,
messy barrack visible to the south. There are two large alcoves to either side
of the burrow; large mounds of furs and pelts piled in these alcoves make for
relatively comfortable bedding. Sharp, polished spears and bucklers made of
tanned hide lean against the western wall. A crude, narrow tunnel leads downward
from the northern end of this burrow.
name: Dark Burrow

desc:
A crude, marble-lined tunnel running in from the north comes to an abrupt
dead-end at a rough granite wall to the south. Notches in the wall show the
locations of unsuccessful attempts to try to break through this hard rock;
broken pickaxes on the floor further testify to this failure. An alternate route
bypassing the granite wall is given in the form of a jagged, crudely-carved hole
in the ceiling which leads upward into a spacious burrow.
name: Roughly-Hewn Tunnel

desc:
A crudely-delved tunnel runs north and south, bulging here where it meets an
underground cavity. This cavity opens east and west, its smoothly layered walls
a stark contrast to the sloppy, uneven surfaces of the tunnel. A faint, acrid
burnt odour lingers in the stale, dry air that fills this underground chamber.
name: Roughly-Hewn Tunnel

desc:
Rows of carcasses hang down from the low ceiling of a broad chamber. Cast-off
mounds of reeking entrails sit in a corner. Piles of skins and furs lie on a
natural rock shelf in the deepest part of the cavern, next to sharp tools that
could be used to fashion them into armour and other garments. The walls of the
cave have been spattered a deep reddish-brown that could only come from blood.
name: Bloody Butchery

desc:
A barred grate separates this cramped cranny from the rest of the cave. The
ceiling slants downward until it meets the floor a few yards beyond the grate.
Cracked bones lie scattered among piles of stale dung and shed fur sits in piles
along the walls.
name: Filthy Pen

desc:
Smooth dolomite makes up the surfaces of a large, circular chamber. The floor is
covered in small piles of ashes and charred coals, and black scorch marks mar
the white walls of the chamber. A crude alcove in the eastern wall holds wood,
torches, and flints - materials for making a fire. A raised platform in the
centre of the chamber is piled with mounds of singed bedding.
name: Scorched Chamber

desc:
Filthy water trickles across the floor of a cave, pooling into stagnant puddles
where there are dips in the rock. Sodden pelts scattered on the floor are
starting to rot; they emit a rank stench. Several giant skeletons rest against
the walls, surrounded by spatters of long-dried blood. The smooth natural walls
of the cavern turn eastward, opening into a grisly scene in the chamber to the
east. However, the trickle of the water continues down a narrow, rubble-lined
mine-tunnel to the north.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
An opaque stream of water flows in from a tunnel to the south. It proceeds to
seep beneath a mound of rubble that entirely covers what would be a gap in the
eastern cave wall. Iron chains extend from rings embedded in the walls, leading
to shackles meant to restrain the slaves that excavated this mine. The dusty
floor is littered with filthy refuse and rotting scraps of food.
name: Collapsed Dead-End

desc:
A narrow valley extends to the west and east, nestled between low mountains to
the north and south. Packed with trees consisting primarily of dark oaks with a
smattering of coniferous types, the shadows from the mountains combine with the
broad, stretching canopy and tangly vines that creep along the floor to create a
deep unending darkness.
name: Forested Valley

desc:
A forested valley is sheltered by low mountains to the north and south as it
dead-ends into a steep cliff wall along the eastern edge. Beneath the forest
canopy, sickly moulds and twisting vines intertwine with one another amidst
great lichen-covered boulders and large slabs of cold granite.
name: Dead-end Wooded Valley

desc:
Gaps among the dark trees reveal that the tangly forest rests atop tall hills
and continues to climb towards the Emyn-nu-Fuin, the Mountains of Mirkwood. As
the forest continues to ascend, the composition of the trees become mainly fir
and other evergreens. The air is noticeably cooler and brisker here than below
in the stuffy depths of the lower woods.
name: Ascending Forest

desc:
A tangled forest spreads across the floor of a valley nestled among the low but
rugged mountains of the Emyn-nu-Fuin. Short and twisted trees with heavy,
lichen-covered branches scrape against the thorny shrubs that grow beneath them.
The slopes of the valley are gentle to the east and the south while the northern
and western slopes are much steeper and covered in outcroppings.
name: Tangled Dell

desc:
The forest in this rocky area grows dense, with a mix of oak, fir and beech
competing for space. Large rocks and boulders covered in blankets of green moss
litter the ground in-between trunks. The land is shrouded in gloom and shadows
as the canopy fights a winning battle of domination over the forest floor
against sun and moon.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
Dark trees and rugged cliffs cover this ridgeline as it extends to the south and
the east. The trees are mostly hoary, gnarled oaks clad in cloaks of mottled
lichen. Green moss grows on the rock faces and on scattered boulders throughout
the region.
name: Twisted Ridgeline

desc:
Some mould-covered boulders sit askew along the apex on this overgrown, rocky
ridge. The mountain range itself is quite low, but steep cliffs and densely
growing thorn bushes make it difficult to traverse. Some trees have managed to
find a home here as well, though they are twisted, stunted things. Dark,
thorn-covered vines hang from branches, swaying gently.
name: Twisted Ridgeline

desc:
A dense forest covers these parts of the Emyn-nu-Fuin. The terrain is broken
with plenty of ravines and jagged, moss-covered boulders. A short but almost
vertical slope to the south leads down into an even denser forest below, while
in other directions, deeper into the mountains, the vegetation thins out.
name: Overgrown Pass

desc:
A dozen large rocks lie in a rough circle around an old campfire. Dark stains
mar the surface of some rocks, and old bones are strewn around these. Old,
rotten pieces of rope hang from the branches of some of the nearest trees. A
layer of decaying leaves hide the ground and a brown-green species of fungi
grows in abundance here.
name: Circle of Stones

desc:
A twisted sprawl of thorny shrubs clings to a narrow, rugged ridge. Several
massive grey boulders rise above the thick black mat to loom over the rocky
terrain. The slopes to either side fall away rapidly, descending into the deep
gloom which inhabits the flanking valleys.
name: Thorny Ridge

desc:
A thick forest of oak and beech covers large, sprawling hills that extend
towards the east. The ground is overgrown with large bushes that surround and
embrace the old, gnarly trunks. The bushes grow so dense and tall that little of
the terrain can be seen in other directions.
name: Sprawling Hills

desc:
Dark vine-entangled trees sweep broadly across tall hilly terrain in complete
darkness under the impregnable canopy looming above. Cracked stones are strewn
throughout the soil amidst massive trunks that dwarf the meagre shrubbery eeking
out a living between their great twisting roots.
name: Tall Hills in a Tangled Forest

desc:
The terrain along the apex of this ridge is bare, jagged stone. There is little
growing here, only some grey, sickly lichen, struggling patches of moss, and
tough weeds. The ridge continues to the east and to the west, while the sides to
the north and to the south drop away in steep cliffs to disappear in the canopy
of Mirkwood below.
name: Twisted Ridgeline

desc:
Jagged rocks line the sides of this twisting ridge. A few short, stunted trees
grow in the stony soil with spiny bushes around them, as if seeking for
protection. The sides of the ridge fall away steeply in cliffs and rubble-strewn
slopes. Passage seems impossible in any direction except following the ridge
towards the east.
name: Twisted Ridgeline

desc:
This sombre hallway has a roughly square cross section; though the walls and
floor are somewhat rough, they are far too smooth to have resulted from natural
processes. A jagged, shadowy gap in the northern wall widens into a rugged,
ascending slope. The gloomy hallway continues to the east and to the west, with
no end in sight in either direction.
name: Nether Route

desc:
A long hallway extending from the east meets an apparent dead-end. The western
wall narrows suddenly, tapering to an end as the walls to the north and the
south squeeze inward. Despite the lack of any obvious continuation of the
hallway, a stale breeze carries a foul stench eastward into the dark gloom of
the corridor.
name: Nether Route

desc:
The floor of this hallway is level and worn smooth. Less care was spent on the
walls, which undulate slightly as the passage runs to the east and the west.
There are some faint markings in the smooth, black rock of the floor. A square
has been carved into the centre of passage, along with an arrow pointing
eastward.
name: Nether Route

desc:
A narrow crawlspace leads westward. The tunnel is further restricted by clusters
of stalactites. Jagged rocks protrude from the walls and holes in the floor
conspire to hinder any further attempt to pass through the tunnel. A foul, acrid
stench wafts down the tunnel from the west.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
Sharp, protruding rocks in the walls make traversing this passage a hazardous
affair. There is little space to manoeuvre or turn around as one moves along
this tunnel, which runs in from the east and sharply climbs upward into a round
hole in the ceiling. A foul white and black substance sifts in from above,
covering the ground and walls of the passage. This layer has recently been
disturbed by a creature navigating this cramped space; it cannot have passed
through here without being coated in the filth.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
A cramped tunnel leads from a small opening below into a broad cavern to the
north. There is little room to navigate, as the ceiling is no more than a few
feet high, and the narrow walls leave no room to manoeuvre. This crawlspace
appears to be natural in origin, though it is unclear what created it. The air
that drifts in from the north carries an acrid stench along.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
Huge slabs of black rock cover the floor of this hallway, each worn smooth. The
walls have been carefully worked to be free of protruding rocks, and the
passageway seems perfectly straight. The ceiling is not very high, perhaps a
deliberate design considering the care taken in the other aspects of this
tunnel. Two massive pillars of rough stone form a frame for an opening to the
south which leads to a steep cave-like passageway; it is a striking contrast to
this corridor.
name: Nether Route

desc:
A dark corridor with a floor of smooth black rock leads into deep gloom to the
east and to the west. It is interrupted only by a narrow opening in its northern
wall where a steep set of stairs ascends to a higher level of these caves. Less
care was taken when crafting the roof than the rest of the hallway, and the
uneven, rugged surface stands in stark contrast to the smooth walls and floor.
name: Nether Route

desc:
This room is a nearly perfect cube, its smooth walls carved with brute force
from the solid rock. The floor has been meticulously flattened and is bare
except for a neatly rolled up pelt in one corner. There are several empty racks
and shelves on the southern wall.
name: Austere Chamber

desc:
A low, narrow corridor meets the top of a set of stairs that descends deep into
the rock. The bottom of the stairs is shrouded in impenetrable gloom. The floor
of the tunnel is worn smooth, as if countless passing feet have treaded upon the
exposed bedrock. To the north, there is an underground junction where several
corridors meet.
name: Atop a Narrow Set of Stairs

desc:
Passages from the south, the east, the west, and above meet at this underground
intersection. These passages are covered with wooden slats which rest atop the
soft dirt of the floors; these tunnels would be suitable for the transport of
heavy loads. This junction is wide and tall with solid walls and a dry ceiling.
name: Underground Crossway

desc:
Bunk beds three and four storeys high line this cramped barracks in long rows
which leave only narrow paths between them. These beds are made of wood, with
rickety frames and thin slats. Several racks and pegs on both sides of the
entrance are used for storing equipment.
name: Orkish Sleeping Quarters

desc:
Many wooden shelves line the walls of this square chamber. In the middle of the
room there is a great pile of sturdy crates. A ladder with wheels stands in the
middle beside the crates. Though there are no useful items on the shelves, dust,
broken pieces, and numerous scraps indicate that this great storeroom was once
filled with supplies and equipment.
name: Crate-filled Storeroom

desc:
The smell of dung, litter, and wet fur hangs in the air of this east-west
corridor. The floor is covered in trampled dirt and dung, a striking contrast to
a smooth and well-maintained corridor that lies through an opening heading
uphill. Numerous foot and paw prints of various creatures crisscross the grimy
floor.
name: Irregular, Malodorous Corridor

desc:
Very little care was used in the burrowing of this corridor. Scattered wooden
beams seem to barely support the ceiling, and the walls are uneven and rough. A
big stack of leaves is piled against the southern wall, next to a solid wooden
trough. The air is stagnant and foul, though the hint of a breeze flows in from
the east.
name: Irregular, Malodorous Corridor

desc:
A corridor entering from the west turns to meet a wooden cage-door to the south.
The corridor seems unfinished, as if the digging was halted halfway through the
task. The walls are uneven, some parts smoothed and level and others jagged and
rough. The ceiling is unsupported, and clods of fallen dirt rest on the floor.
The door to the south has just enough space to open fully, and not an inch more.
name: Irregular, Malodorous Corridor

desc:
The walls and floor of this corridor tell a tale of crude craftsmanship, based
more on brute force than skill. A strong acrid smell fills the air. The corridor
continues to the east while simple steps carved from the bedrock ascend to
another cavern.
name: Irregular, Malodorous Corridor

desc:
This small cave is used to train and handle bats. A large cage to the south has
a filthy floor covered with guano. Bits of fur poke up from the guano, though
discerning whether just tufts of fur or buried bat corpses would require a
closer investigation. Several wires spanning the cave just beneath the ceiling
have butchered bat corpses dangling from them. On a blood-smeared table in a
corner lie several small knives and other metal instruments in a disordered
heap, but also some small jars with berries.
name: Bat Trainer's Cave

desc:
Rows of wooden spars stretch from wall to wall just below the ceiling, forming
an intersecting network. A pungent, acrid smell fills the cage, its source
evidently the piles of bat guano on the floor. There is an opening to the
outside world in the southern wall, but it is blocked by a sturdy grille made of
narrow wooden bars.
name: Spacious Cage

desc:
A shadowed ledge has been made into a cage intended for holding animals. The
southern wall is open to the outside aside from solid wooden bars, permitting
fresh mountain breezes to enter the cage. A stout wooden door blocks a passage
into the mountain wall to the north; the door is scarred with claw marks. A
stack of leaves and twigs at the eastern wall serves as bedding for the animals.
name: Wolf Cage

desc:
A number of short fences partition this massive cave. Leather muzzles and
various kinds of whips hang from hooks and pegs on the western wall. There is a
large drinking trough next to the wall under the hooks, and fresh meat is stored
in a wooden crate next to it.
name: Warg Training Ground

desc:
This is a spacious cave with shallow burrows in the wall which contain small
piles of leaves. A grille on the southern wall offers a draft of fresh air into
the cave. On the ground, there are some cracked bones that still have some
scraps of flesh on them. There are also several large logs with deep teeth
marks. Apparently, this is a holding pen for wild animals.
name: Wolf Cage

desc:
This chamber stands witness to a ghastly slaughter. Dark red splotches stain the
walls and floor. Large bones are scattered across the ground, none arranged in
any semblance of skeleton. Split and shattered skulls testify to the sheer
brutality of butchery that occurred here. A crudely-carved tunnel leads downward
out of this macabre scene; it seems those who delved here did not bother to
clear the carnage.
name: Gruesome Cavern

desc:
A crude tunnel cuts through dark-grey marble, slanting downward from a
blood-spattered chamber above it. The walls of the tunnel are coarse and rough,
and pocked with many natural cavities and crevices in the rock. The tunnel
levels out and broadens to the south.
name: Rough-Hewn Tunnel

desc:
Piles of supplies line the walls of this dolomite cave. Large, open barrels
holding clear water sit on rough shelves carved into the rock. Crates filled
with mundane tools such as picks and hammers fill an alcove near the entrance of
the chamber. Tanned hides hang from the ceiling, and slats of wood rest against
the walls.
name: Cramped Storage Cave

desc:
This large hall is equipped with a table and two beds. The walls look finely cut
and the floor is made of wooden boards except for the area near the southern
exit which has been left untouched. The ceiling has large wooden beams going
across from wall to wall.
name: Hidden Guardpost

desc:
A cleft in the rock emerges suddenly onto a large, flat overhang. The stream
that courses through the crevice broadens into a wet, slippery film before it
cascades over the ledge. Below lies a broad valley, blackened as if ravaged by a
raging fire.
name: Ledge above an Outpost

desc:
Heaping mounds of trash fill a recess in a cliff wall. Most of the junk that
lies here is soft, such as rotten boughs, mouldy furs, and putrefying entrails.
However, several rusted weapons and bits of armour are scattered through the
refuse, and a huge spider carcass lies at the back of the cleft. Swarms of
buzzing flies hover over the garbage, enticed by the noxious stench.
name: Pile of Rubbish

desc:
Mud and sodden vegetation cover this steep slope. Trash is piled at the foot of
the slope and a fall down the slope might be softened by the garbage. The top of
the slope is no more than fifteen feet away but reaching it is in all likelihood
an impossibility.
name: Slippery Slope

desc:
Dust and debris lie scattered about this chamber, likely originating from a
gaping rent in the ceiling. Water percolates from under a large mound of rock
along the western wall of the cavern. It flows through the centre before
disappearing into a narrow crevice in the deepest recesses of the cave.
name: Rubble-Strewn Chamber
